text:
KINGSTON, Jamaica— Jamaica finished fifth in the mixed 4x400m relays on Saturday’s third day of the track and field schedule at the Olympic Games at Stade de France in Paris running 3:11.67.

The performance was an improvement on their seventh place finish three years ago in Tokyo, Japan.

The team initially finished in sixth place but was moved up a position after France was disqualified.

The Jamaicans fielded an unchanged team from the one that qualified on Friday with Reheem Hayles, Junelle Bromfield, Zandrion Barnes and Stephenie Ann McPherson battling a powerful field.

They ran slower than the national record 3:11.06 seconds.

A majestic anchor leg run from superstar Femke Bol who got the baton in fourth place, took the Netherlands team to victory in a European Area Record 3:07.43 seconds, ahead of the USA who set a world record on Friday with Great Britain third in 3:08.01 seconds.

-Paul A Reid
